intro: Napaea divaricata, also known as Napaea, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, native to Central and South America. Traditionally, Napaea has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Napaea include napaeine and napaline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Napaea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often blended with other herbs to create remedies for colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
